task_completion.description: The agent opened the vip_list.txt file to identify the VIP email addresses.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or file viewer displaying the contents of 'vip_list.txt' with a list of email addresses.

task_completion.description: A new folder named 'VIP-Accounts' was created in the email client.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The BlueMail interface shows a folder named 'VIP-Accounts' in the folder list or sidebar.

task_completion.description: Emails from the VIP addresses were moved into the 'VIP-Accounts' folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'VIP-Accounts' folder is open in BlueMail, and the message list displays emails originating from the VIP senders.

task_completion.description: A draft email is created with the correct recipient and subject line.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A compose window in BlueMail showing 'director@company.com' in the 'To' field and 'VIP Account Status Update' in the 'Subject' field.

task_completion.description: The draft email body contains a list of the subject lines from the isolated VIP emails.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The compose window's text body contains a list of email subject lines that correspond to the emails moved into the VIP-Accounts folder.

integrity.description: The agent used the BlueMail GUI to manage folders and draft the email.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the BlueMail application interface rather than terminal commands manipulating Maildir files directly.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass GUI interactions for email management.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing commands like `mkdir`, `mv`, or `echo` being used to create mail folders, move messages, or generate draft files.

integrity.description: The draft email is genuinely composed within the email client.
integrity.visual_evidence: The draft is visible in the BlueMail compose window, not merely written in a standalone text editor or terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The VIP list is located at /home/ga/Documents/vip_list.txt. The target folder to be created in BlueMail is exactly 'VIP-Accounts'. The draft email must be addressed to 'director@company.com' with the subject 'VIP Account Status Update'. The body of the draft must contain the subject lines of the isolated VIP emails.
